items.title: Records and entities: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Any values frequently used together should be copied into one record, including every derived and unrelated field.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Group cohesive facts about one entity, name their meanings and constraints, and derive redundant values rather than storing conflicting copies when possible.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Group cohesive facts about one entity, name their meanings and constraints, and derive redundant values rather than storing conflicting copies when possible.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Records and entities instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Group cohesive facts about one entity, name their meanings and constraints, and derive redundant values rather than storing conflicting copies when possible.
